# Auto-Generated Memory Coverage Expansion Plan

**Goal:** Expand `msync` to scan all Claude Code auto-generated memory locations, while excluding user-authored files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, etc.).

**Architecture:** Keep the single-file CLI structure. Expand `scanMemories()` to cover additional auto-generated memory directories. No adapter registry or multi-file refactor — the tool is 290 lines and should stay simple.

---

## Scope Boundary

### Include (all auto-generated by Claude Code)

| Source | Location | Format |
|--------|----------|--------|
| Project auto memory | `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md` | Markdown with frontmatter |
| Project auto memory index | `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/MEMORY.md` | Markdown (currently skipped — bug) |
| Subagent memory (user scope) | `~/.claude/agent-memory/*/*.md` | Markdown |
| Subagent memory (project scope) | `<project>/.claude/agent-memory/*/*.md` | Markdown |
| Subagent memory (local scope) | `<project>/.claude/agent-memory-local/*/*.md` | Markdown |
| Custom memory directory | Via `autoMemoryDirectory` in settings | Markdown |

### Exclude

- `CLAUDE.md`, `CLAUDE.local.md` — user-authored project instructions
- `AGENTS.md`, `GEMINI.md` — user-authored agent/tool instructions
- `.cursorrules`, `.windsurfrules`, `.clinerules` — user-authored
- `copilot-instructions.md` — user-authored
- Session transcripts (`*.jsonl`) — raw conversation logs, too noisy
- `history.jsonl` — command history, not structured memory
- Dreams — not a separate store, consolidates existing memory in-place

### Rule for ambiguous sources

If a file cannot be confirmed as auto-generated, exclude it.

## Research Findings

### Bug: MEMORY.md skipped

Current code at `cli.js:110`:
```js
if (!f.endsWith('.md') || f === 'MEMORY.md' || f.startsWith('.')) continue
```
This skips `MEMORY.md`, which is the **index file** — often the most important file in the memory directory. It contains pointers and one-line summaries of all topic memories. Must be included.

### Missing: Subagent Memory (3 scopes)

Claude Code subagents can have persistent memory enabled. When active, they auto-generate markdown memory files in three locations:

1. **User scope** `~/.claude/agent-memory/<agent-name>/` — persists across all projects
2. **Project scope** `<project>/.claude/agent-memory/<agent-name>/` — committed to repo
3. **Local scope** `<project>/.claude/agent-memory-local/<agent-name>/` — gitignored

Each contains a `MEMORY.md` index and optional topic files, same format as project memory.

### Missing: autoMemoryDirectory setting

Claude Code supports `autoMemoryDirectory` in settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` or project-level). When set, the memory directory is redirected. Current code hardcodes `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/`.

### Third-party memory systems (deferred)

OpenMemory/Letta/Zep are primarily API-backed services without stable local file stores. Building adapters now would be premature — they'd be detection-only scaffolds with no real ingestion. Defer until these systems expose documented local export formats.

## Detailed Tasks

### Task 1: Fix MEMORY.md inclusion

**File:** `cli.js`

- [ ] Remove `f === 'MEMORY.md'` from the skip condition
- [ ] Verify: `node cli.js --list` shows MEMORY.md entries

### Task 2: Add subagent memory scanning

**File:** `cli.js`

- [ ] Scan `~/.claude/agent-memory/*/` for user-scope subagent memories
  - Each subdirectory is an agent name
  - Read all `.md` files inside (including `MEMORY.md`)
  - Set `project` to the agent name, prefixed to distinguish (e.g., `agent:code-reviewer`)

- [ ] Scan project-scope and local-scope subagent memories
  - For each project in `~/.claude/projects/*/`, reverse the project key to find the project directory
  - Check `<project-dir>/.claude/agent-memory/*/` and `<project-dir>/.claude/agent-memory-local/*/`
  - This requires reversing the project key encoding (current `projectName()` does partial reversal already)

- [ ] In the picker, group subagent memories separately from project memories
  - Use a label like `agent:<agent-name>` or `<project>/agent:<agent-name>`

### Task 3: Support autoMemoryDirectory

**File:** `cli.js`

- [ ] Read `~/.claude/settings.json` for `autoMemoryDirectory`
- [ ] If set, use it as an additional or replacement scan root
- [ ] Also check project-level `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json`

### Task 4: Update README

**File:** `README.md`

- [ ] Document all scanned locations
- [ ] List excluded file types

## Verification

- [ ] `node cli.js --list` shows MEMORY.md when present
- [ ] Subagent memories appear in the picker under their agent name
- [ ] User-authored files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) are never included
- [ ] Custom `autoMemoryDirectory` is respected when configured
- [ ] Tool still works correctly when subagent memory dirs don't exist

## Deferred Work

- Third-party memory adapters (OpenMemory, Letta, Zep) — add when they have documented local file formats
- `--json` output for programmatic access
- MCP memory server integration
